5.description: Optimal disclosures in dimensions c and r, respectively This figure illustrates how optimal disclosures in either dimension c or dimension r evolve with the deterioration of the average quality of the banking system, represented by a reduction in qc. Panels A to C illustrate optimal disclosures in dimension c with the corresponding distributions of scores, and panels D to F illustrate those in dimension r. In each panel, the horizontal axis represents disclosed scores, and the vertical axis represents the mass of banks; solid areas represent ‚Äúpreserved‚ÄĚ banks when őłőł=őł^‚Ā†, with their total mass represented by the w in the title, and hollow areas represent ‚Äúsacrificed‚ÄĚ banks. For all panels, (r_,r¬Į)=(2,3)‚Ā†, qr=0.5,‚ÄČ(c_,c¬Į)=(3.75,5.75)‚Ā†, a(l)=2‚ąíl,‚ÄČőł^=9‚Ā†, and both c¬Į‚ąíc_>[A(qc)qc‚ąíA(1)‚ąíA(qc)1‚ąíqc]Er and r¬Į/r_>A(qr)qr1‚ąíqrA(1)‚ąíA(qr) are satisfied.

6.description: The minimum mass of type-c_banks The black solid curves in this figure illustrate the minimum mass Qc of type-c_ banks among banks of a given mass W that can keep them from runs under optimal disclosures in dimension c when őłőł=őł^‚Ā†, given by x^c(W,Qc,A(¬∑))=őł^‚Ā†. The horizontal axis of each panel represents the mass W of all banks, and the vertical axis represents the mass Qc of type-c_ banks. The blue dashed lines indicate the natural constraint Qc‚Č§W‚Ā†. For all panels, (r_,r¬Į)=(2,3)‚Ā†, Er=2.5‚Ā†, and őł^=9‚Ā†. From panels A to C, we scale up a(¬∑) to achieve higher systemic risks. To ensure the feasibility of the same őł^=9 for disclosures under a uniformly higher systemic risk a(¬∑)‚Ā†, we lower (c_,c¬Į) accordingly: we pick c¬Į=9.5‚ąí1.5√óEr√óa(l)2‚ąíl and c_=c¬Į‚ąí2‚Ā†.
